prose:
Method provenance and results
Property: Atom-condensed Fukui functions (f⁺ nucleophilic attack susceptibility, f⁻ electrophilic attack susceptibility, f⁰ radical susceptibility) and dual descriptor for benzaldehyde; identification of the most electrophilic (highest f⁺) and most nucleophilic (highest f⁻) atomic sites.
Level of theory: DFT, functional B3LYP with def2-TZVP basis set (tier “standard”); exact (no density-fitting) RKS implementation in PySCF via chemkit.
Geometry provenance: single-point evaluation on the supplied Cartesian coordinates (file “/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/benzaldehyde/benzaldehyde.xyz”).  No geometry optimisation was performed (DFT//input-XYZ).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation model).
Charge and multiplicity: neutral singlet parent state (charge 0, multiplicity 1).  N–1 cation and N+1 anion states were computed as doublets (charge +1/−1, multiplicity 2) to evaluate finite-difference Fukui indices.
Software: PySCF (backend used by chemkit’s “fukui-reactivity” skill).
Convergence & diagnostics: SCF cycles for all three charge states converged; chemkit’s integrity check reports status = ok, trustworthy = true.  No warnings were reported by the engine.
Live log & files: the calculation streamed its progress to a live “fukui_*.out” file in the working directory (path recorded in the run header); the result JSON is at “/home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmphnazrce6/live.json” and the bar-chart PNG at “/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/benzaldehyde/argo_o3/20260704-185546_benzaldehyde_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png”.

Key results (per-atom indices; labels follow the input order):
• Most electrophilic site (largest f⁺): atom 3 (C, carbonyl carbon)  f⁺ = 0.39938967  (dimensionless)
• Most nucleophilic site (largest f⁻): atom 1 (O, carbonyl oxygen)  f⁻ = 0.23839906
Sum checks: Σf⁺ = 1.0000, Σf⁻ = 1.0000 (as expected for finite-difference definition).

The headline value returned under “value” is the maximum electrophilic Fukui index f⁺ = 0.39938967 (atom 3), reported exactly as produced by the chemkit tool.

engine integrity.trustworthy verdict: true
